preK.definition: It's a little sad feeling you get when something you hoped for doesn't happen.
preK.example: I felt a little disappointment when my friend couldn't come over to play today.
preK.tryIt: What might make you feel a little disappointment?

K.definition: It's when you feel a little sad because something you really hoped for didn't happen the way you wanted.
K.example: Lily felt **disappointment** when her balloon popped before she could show it to her friends.
K.tryIt: Think of a time you were really hoping for a sunny day to play outside, but it rained instead. That's a feeling of disappointment!

G1.definition: It's a sad feeling you get when something you hoped for or really wanted to happen doesn't happen.
G1.example: I felt disappointment when it started to rain and we couldn't go to the park.
G1.tryIt: What would make you feel disappointment? Maybe if your friend couldn't come over to play?

preK.definition: Safety means being careful so you don't get hurt. Like wearing your helmet!
preK.example: Wearing a helmet helps keep you safe when you ride your scooter or tricycle.
preK.tryIt: What helps keep the child's head safe when they ride the scooter?

K.definition: Safety means staying safe and not getting hurt. Wearing your helmet helps with safety!
K.example: Putting on a helmet before riding the scooter is good safety.
K.tryIt: Why do you think the child is wearing a helmet?

G1.definition: Safety means being careful so you don't get hurt, like wearing your helmet.
G1.example: The child puts on their helmet for safety before riding the scooter.
G1.tryIt: Why do you think wearing a helmet is a safe thing to do when riding a scooter or tricycle?
